structural_claim: Symmetry breaking is a fundamental organizing principle where systems spontaneously select lower-symmetry states from higher-symmetry possibilities, creating emergent structure and properties

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Phase transitions are symmetry-breaking events where order parameters emerge, with symmetry as the fundamental organizing principle

supporting_results.result_name: Discovery of the W and Z bosons
supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Electroweak symmetry is spontaneously broken, generating massive gauge bosons from a symmetric theory
A scalar field with non-zero vacuum expectation value breaks electroweak symmetry and generates mass
